title: Identify geometric solids (3D shapes)
description: Identify prisms, pyramids, cylinders, cones, and spheres.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the 3D shape shown below.** 


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A **cone** has one circular base joined to a curved side that ends in a  point.

The shape above has two circular bases.  So, the shape is not a cone.

A **sphere** is a round solid figure with every point on its surface equidistant from its center.

The shape above has points that are closer and farther from its center.  So, the shape is not a sphere.

A **cylinder** has two parallel circular bases connected by a curved surface.

The shape above matches this description.
The shape is a cylinder.

options.alt: A figure with two parallel circular bases connected by a curved surface. The hidden edges are dashed lines. The figure is taller than it is wide.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the 3D shape shown below.** 


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A **cone** has one circular base joined to a curved side that ends in a  point.

The shape above has two circular bases.  So, the shape is not a cone.

A **rectangular prism** has two parallel rectangular bases connected by  rectangular faces.

The shape above does not have rectangular bases.  So, the shape is not a rectangular prism.

A **cylinder** has two parallel circular bases connected by a curved surface.

The shape above matches this description.
The shape is a cylinder.

options.alt: A figure with two parallel circular bases connected by a curved surface. The hidden edges are dashed lines. The figure is taller than it is wide.

The shape above has two circular bases.  So, the shape is not a cone.

The shape above has only one circular base.  So, the shape is not a cylinder.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the 3D shape shown below.** 


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A **cylinder** has two parallel circular bases connected by a curved surface.

The shape above has only one circular base.  So, the shape is not a cylinder.

A **triangular prism** has two parallel triangular bases connected by rectangular faces.

The shape above does not have triangular bases or rectangular faces. So, the shape is not a triangular prism.

A **cone** has one circular base joined to a curved side that ends in a  point.

The shape above matches this description.
The shape is a cone.

options.alt: A figure with a circular base joined to a curved side that ends in a point. The hidden edges are dashed lines. The figure is wider than it is tall.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the 3D shape shown below.** 


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A **square pyramid** has a square base and triangular faces.

The shape above does not have  triangular faces.  So, the shape is not a square pyramid.

A **triangular prism** has two parallel triangular bases connected by rectangular faces.

The shape above does not have triangular bases.  So, the shape is not a triangular prism.

A **rectangular prism** has two parallel rectangular bases connected by  rectangular faces.

The shape above matches this description.
The shape is a rectangular prism.

options.alt: A figure with two parallel rectangular bases connected by rectangular faces. The figure is the same height and width.

The shape above does not have a  circular base.  So, the shape is not a cone.

A **square pyramid** has a square base and triangular faces.

The shape is a square pyramid.
